name: Salt Lake City to Detroit LTL
hero-headline: Salt Lake City, UT to Detroit, MI LTL Freight
subheadline: 1,756 miles. 5-7 business day transit. 9 carriers active on this lane.
body-content:
Lane Overview: Salt Lake City to Detroit

The Salt Lake City to Detroit LTL lane covers approximately 1,756 miles between the Mountain and Midwest regions. Standard transit runs 5-7 business days depending on carrier routing and terminal schedules. Common equipment on this corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Cross-dock facilities at Denver Crossroads, Phoenix Distribution, Inland Empire support consolidation and transit optimization. Winter weather impacts mountain passes Dec-Mar. Steady otherwise.

How WARP Operates the Salt Lake City to Detroit Lane

WARP covers the Salt Lake City to Detroit corridor with 9 vetted carriers providing LTL capacity.

  • Service capabilities on this lane include: Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery; Estimated 5-7 business day transit window; Pallet-level tracking with delivery appointment scheduling.
  • Shipments receive pallet-level tracking from pickup through delivery with appointment scheduling and delivery confirmation.
  • Nearby cross-dock operations at Denver Crossroads and Phoenix Distribution enable consolidation and re-routing when exceptions occur.
  • Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change.
  • WARP's operations team manages carrier coordination, rescheduling, and resolution without requiring shipper follow-up.

Operating Details

Key operational considerations for this lane:

  • Estimated lane distance: 1,756 miles (Salt Lake City to Detroit)
  • Transit window: 5-7 business days for standard LTL service
  • Available equipment: Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled)
  • Active carriers on this lane: 9
  • Nearest cross-dock facilities: Denver Crossroads, Phoenix Distribution, Inland Empire, Columbus Terminal, Chicago Gateway
  • Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery milestones
  • Exception alerts within 30 minutes of status changes with automated escalation
  • Delivery appointment scheduling and confirmation managed by WARP operations

Pricing & Rate Factors

Estimated LTL rates on the Salt Lake City to Detroit lane range from $4,372 to $5,829, depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and seasonal demand.

  • Key cost drivers include NMFC freight classification, shipment density, and whether accessorials such as liftgate, inside delivery, or limited-access pickup apply.
  • At 1,756 miles, the linehaul distance is the largest cost component (approximately $2.
  • 90/mile).
  • Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item based on current diesel prices.
  • These are modeled estimates.

Enter your shipment details to get a real-time, all-in rate.

Validate This Lane

Validate this lane with a controlled pilot: Salt Lake City to Detroit LTL (1,756-mile corridor; 5–7 day transit window; 9 vetted carriers active; cross-dock access at Denver Crossroads and Phoenix Distribution). Track quote response time, transit predictability, and exception trends before scaling volume. Start with a single shipment to benchmark WARP against your current process.

faq-schema:
How much does LTL freight from Salt Lake City, UT to Detroit, MI cost?

Estimated LTL rates range from $4,372 to $5,829 based on freight class, weight, and pallet count. Actual rates depend on shipment specifics and current market conditions. Get an instant quote for real-time pricing.

What is the transit time for LTL shipments from Salt Lake City to Detroit?

Standard LTL transit on this 1,756-mile lane is 5-7 business days. Actual transit depends on carrier routing, terminal schedules, and pickup timing.

What carriers does WARP use on the Salt Lake City to Detroit lane?

WARP maintains a vetted network of approximately 9 active carriers on this corridor. Carrier selection is optimized for each shipment based on equipment requirements, transit targets, and performance history.

Can I track my LTL shipment from Salt Lake City to Detroit in real time?

Yes. Every shipment includes real-time tracking with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change, and WARP's operations team manages resolution directly.

What equipment is available for LTL freight on this lane?

Common equipment on the Salt Lake City to Detroit corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Equipment availability varies by season and demand. Specify your requirements when requesting a quote.
